comments.author: @dspychalski
comments.text: I have lived here for 5 years, and the people are 95% introverts, total hypocrites, and as self-serving as can be. The pollution cant be terrible, the motorcycles are not kept tuned up according to the law - but police just hang around at the station and only go out when necessary. RUDE drivers, dangerous drivers...total morons. 3,000 traffic deaths a year, and 2000 of them are from motorcyclists. These are the facts...yes, they can be friendly, but only because they want to 'appear' nice...out of duty, in public...to look good for others. Toaslly judgemental country. It is beautiful in many, many places...and green as can be. They take care of their land...but not each other.

comments.author: @suanna7870
comments.text:
It is a fact that Taiwanese people are not Chinese. Taiwanese are indigenous peoples, comprising 16 ethnic groups belonging to the Austronesian language family, including the Amis, Atayal, Paiwan, Bunun, Puyuma, Rukai, Tsou, Saisiyat, Yami (Tao), Thao, Kavalan ＇, Taroko, Sakizaya, Seediq, Kanakanapu, and La'alua, among others. They are distributed across New Zealand, Australia, New Guinea, Java, and Southeast Asian countries.

The island's indigenous people have intermarried with people from various countries around the world, including the Netherlands, Japan, China (during the Ming and Qing dynasties), and a small number of families and veterans of the Kuomintang who retreated to Taiwan.

Culturally, Taiwan boasts a diverse array of cultures from around the world, as well as a rich variety of languages ​​and cuisines, making it a food paradise. It also possesses diverse humanities and geographical characteristics, with frigid, subtropical, and tropical environments, and a beautiful natural setting. It is a beautiful island nation, a sovereign and independent country belonging to the Austronesian language family. Its people cherish freedom, love, peace, and democracy. We welcome people from all over the world to visit Taiwan. Thank you, and best wishes to everyone. ❤❤❤👍👍🎉

comments.author: @scavengerl9827
comments.text: Every places get their good and bad. Taiwan is a place with smart hard working people. However, decades of misdirected democracy led Taiwan to its own spiral down. Beat up buildings and ill-designed city planning, pedestrian hell, polarization of wealth, unattainable housing price, low birth rate, and younger generation sees no hope in their future. Anxiety has been built up in a seemingly peaceful surface.

The situation described applies to lots of countries worldwide; not just Taiwan.

In fact, Taiwan’s living conditions and infrastructure vastly improved after democratization!

comments.text:
Taiwan is a province of the Republic of China 🇹🇼 , never was ruled by the Chinese Communists regime.
Taiwan was lucky to be protected by the courageous and determined freedom fighters of the Military of the Republic of China on Taiwan 🇹🇼 after the central government of R.O.C. retreated to Taiwan in 1949 from mainland China. 
Thank you for sharing the real history about Taiwan. Thanks to all pioneers of the beautiful island Taiwan (Formosa) who also shared their excellent resilience. 
I'm very proud of my beloved native country the Republic of China 🇹🇼 on Taiwan and I love her forever. ❤️ 😊 👍
